text:
e Atmospheric Ketus are those that are sighted on flagstaff's,
weapons, buildings, trees, horses, elephants and-vuch other quadru-
peds. The Celestial ones are seen amidst the constellations, and the
Terrestrial ones are those that do not come under either of the two
categories.
sjwteita 11*11
Some Sages like Parasara speak of .101 Ketus, while others
such as Garga of 1000 Ketus. However, Sage Narada declares that
there is only one Ketu which appears in many forms and places.
[According to TTnrr there are 101 Ketus, of which 16 were
born of the breath of Death, 12 of the Sun, 10 of RudraJs wrath at
the destruction of Daksa's sacrifice, 7 of Pitamaha (creator), 15
are the sons of Sage Uddalaka, 17 were born of the foreheads of
Sagei Marici and Kasyapa, 6 of Prajapati^s laughter, 3 of fire, 1 of
smoke, 14 were born along with the Moon when the ocean was
churned for nectar, and lastly one was born of Brahman's anger.
